title: NGE vs NGE Directors Cut 22 the mindrape scene
display_title: NGE vs NGE Directors Cut 22 the mindrape scene
wikitext:
[]: NGE vs. NGE Director's Cut 22 - the mindrape scene             Quote Post by trueno_twentyone » Mon Oct 12, 2009 10:28 pm I was wondering which was better. I haven't seen DC in my entire life and I am hoping to see it someday. But for now, the question lies as it is. Which scene is better. We can also answer which is better NGE or NGE Director's Cut?

[]:             Quote Post by Great Genius Shinji-Sama » Mon Oct 12, 2009 10:38 pm IMO Mindfuck DC is better because it digs deeper into Asuka's psyche her feelings for baka-chan her moms suicide and a lot of the stuff the on air or normal version doesnt show you. But except for the first few episodes of the last disk theres not really any difference between NGE or DC IMO.

[]:             Quote Post by Eva Yojimbo » Mon Oct 12, 2009 11:12 pm The DCs are better in all respects, period.

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Tue Oct 13, 2009 1:36 am Overall, the DC material is 50% about what's really going on and 50% about Asuka. So in episode 22' she gets the extended treatment -- including a sequence in which she seems to fear that Kaji is actually after Shinji. (Thank you, Rebuild 2.0, for clarifying that snippet!)

[]:             Quote Post by GasmaskAvenger » Tue Oct 13, 2009 4:03 am the director's cut episodes feel more complete when you see them

[]: Re: NGE vs. NGE Director's Cut 22 - the mindrape scene             Quote Post by Orphan Of Darkness » Tue Oct 13, 2009 5:05 am trueno_twentyone wrote: I was wondering which was better. I haven't seen DC in my entire life and I am hoping to see it someday. But for now, the question lies as it is. Which scene is better. We can also answer which is better NGE or NGE Director's Cut?

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Tue Oct 13, 2009 8:44 am Mr. Tines wrote: including a sequence in which she seems to fear that Kaji is actually after Shinji.

[]: Re: NGE vs. NGE Director's Cut 22 - the mindrape scene             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Tue Oct 13, 2009 9:00 am Eva Yojimbo wrote: The DCs are better in all respects, period.

[]:             Quote Post by carla » Tue Oct 13, 2009 9:26 am Mr. Tines wrote: including a sequence in which she seems to fear that Kaji is actually after Shinji.

[]:             Quote Post by MatrixRefugee » Tue Oct 13, 2009 11:06 am Mr. Tines wrote: Overall, the DC material is 50% about what's really going on and 50% about Asuka. So in episode 22' she gets the extended treatment -- including a sequence in which she seems to fear that Kaji is actually after Shinji. (Thank you, Rebuild 2.0, for clarifying that snippet!)

[]:             Quote Post by Marzan » Tue Oct 13, 2009 11:53 am DC definitely has a more complete feeling to it. It gives a greater insight into Asuka that helped me understand the story better

[]:             Quote Post by MatrixRefugee » Tue Oct 13, 2009 11:57 am Marzan wrote: DC definitely has a more complete feeling to it. It gives a greater insight into Asuka that helped me understand the story better

[]:             Quote Post by Joseph the PRPD » Tue Oct 13, 2009 2:10 pm carla wrote: Mr. Tines wrote: including a sequence in which she seems to fear that Kaji is actually after Shinji.

[]:             Quote Post by Rabid-Sachiel » Tue Oct 13, 2009 3:40 pm I say the DC, as it has more scenes and is more complete, like what some others said before. I.e. episode 24, when it shows Misato looking at Kaworu form a distance, and Kaworu talking to SEELE.

[]:             Quote Post by LiLi » Tue Oct 13, 2009 5:48 pm That's probably just me, but I tend to think of the Video' (aka DC) episodes as an alternate continuity to go with the EoE finale (25' and 26'). IIRC, the extra scenes were mostly originally made for Death, which was originally supposed to be the beginning of the Evangelion movie. Both continuities (ON AIR and VIDEO) have their limits and their merits in my eyes. I am partial to certain aspects of the ON AIR continuity, but I can enjoy the other one as well... precisely because they are different. The fact some of the uglier animation sequences from the TV version were eventually fixed in both versions on DVD helps, too.

[]:             Quote Post by oOoOoOo » Tue Oct 13, 2009 7:34 pm The DC version is the closest you'll get to the inside of my mind. I suppose that's exciting or disturbing, depending on your worldview. ^^; Any "non-anime fan" I've shown NGE to really loved the Asuka mindrape scene, and although they didn't have the original TV version to compare, it is safe to say that it really moves people viscerally.

[]:             Quote Post by Xard » Wed Oct 14, 2009 10:06 am how the fuck someone in here haven't seen DCs? What the fuck they are infinitely superior every single time. Get them already, GEEZ

[]: Re: NGE vs. NGE Director's Cut 22 - the mindrape scene             Quote Post by supershinjiasukashipper » Wed Oct 14, 2009 1:56 pm trueno_twentyone wrote: I was wondering which was better. I haven't seen DC in my entire life and I am hoping to see it someday. But for now, the question lies as it is. Which scene is better. We can also answer which is better NGE or NGE Director's Cut?

[]: Re: NGE vs. NGE Director's Cut 22 - the mindrape scene             Quote Post by supershinjiasukashipper » Wed Oct 14, 2009 1:56 pm trueno_twentyone wrote: I was wondering which was better. I haven't seen DC in my entire life and I am hoping to see it someday. But for now, the question lies as it is. Which scene is better. We can also answer which is better NGE or NGE Director's Cut?

[]:             Quote Post by MatrixRefugee » Wed Oct 14, 2009 2:16 pm Xard wrote: how the fuck someone in here haven't seen DCs? What the fuck they are infinitely superior every single time. Get them already, GEEZ
